sutta: monks i will teach you how to make become the 5-limbed aryan right concentration listen attentively and pay heed and i will speak even so lord they replied and the exalted 1 said monks take the case of a monk who secluded from sen's desires secluded from unwholesome mental states enters and abides in the first jhana he steeps and wrenches and fills and suffuses this body with delight and pleasure born of seclusion so that there is not 1 particle of the body that is not pervaded by this delight and pleasure born of seclusion monks just as a handy bathman or a tendon might throw bath powder in some copper basin and gradually sprinkling water kneaded together so that the ball the bath ball gathered up by the moisture became enveloped in moisture and saturated both in and out but did not lose moisture even so among steeps branches fills and suffuses this body with delight and pleasure born of seclusion so that there is not 1 particle of the body that is not pervaded by this delight and pleasure born of seclusion monks this is firstly how to make become the 5 limbed aryan right concentration all among stealing applied and sustained thought enters and abides in the second ghana he likewise steeps this body with delight and pleasure etc monks imagine a pool with a spring with no water inlet either on the east side or on the west or on the north or on the south and suppose the deva supply not proper rains from time to time cool waters would still well up from that pool and that pool would be steeped drenched filled and suffused with the cold water so that every part would be pervaded by the cold water in just the same way monks among steeps his body with delight and pleasure monks this is the secondly how to make become the 5 limbed aryan right concentration again a monk free from the father of delight enters and abides in the third ghana he steeps and drenches and fuels and suffuses this body with a dela with a pleasure divested of delight so that there is not 1 particle of the body that is not pervaded by this pleasure divested of delight lungs just as in the pawn of blue white and red water lilies the plants are born in water grow in water come not out of the water but sunk in the depths fine nourishment and from tip to root are steeped drenched filled and subfused with cold water so that not a part of them is not pervaded by cold water even so amongst among steeps his body with a pleasure divested of delight etc monks this is thirdly how to make become the 5 limbed are in right concentration again among putting away pleasure enters and abides in the fourth jana seated he suffuses his body with brightness by the pureness of his mind so that there is not 1 particle of the body that is not pervaded with brightness by the pureness of his mind monks just as a man might sit with his head downwards covered with a white cloth so that not a portion of it was not in was not covered with that white cloth even so amongst its suffusing his body with brightness monks this importantly how to make become the 5-limbed aryan right concentration again the contemplation sign is rightly grasped by a monk rightly held by the attention rightly reflected upon rightly penetrated by inside monks just as someone might survey another standing might survey another sitting or sitting might survey another lying down even so the contemplation sign is rightly grasped by the monk rightly held by the attention rightly rightly reflected upon rightly rightly penetrated by inside monks this is fifthly how to make become the 5 limbed aren't right concentration monks when a man has thus made become and thus made abundant the 5 limbed aryan concentration he can bend his mind to realize by higher knowledge whatever condition is so realizable and become an eyewitness in every case whatever the range may be suppose a water jar brim full of water so that a crow could drink from it was set on a dish as soon as a strong man rocked it to and fro would the water spill yes lord even so monks when the monk has been so made become as made abundant the 5 limbed are in right concentration he can bend the mind to knowledge whatever the range may be monks imagine a tank on a level piece of ground with dikes built up on its 4 sides brim full of water so that a crow could drink from it as soon as a strong man loosened the sights here and there would the water flow out yes lord even so monks when among his so made become concentration he can bend the mind to knowledge whatever the range may be monks suppose a carriage harnessed with thoroughbreds with gold hanging handy worth to stand on the level where 4 main roads meet as soon as the coachman a capable trainer and driver of horses had mounted and grasped the reins in his left hand and with his right seize the goat he would drive on or back as and how he pleased even so monks when among has so made become and made abundant the 5 limbed are in right concentration he can bend the mind to realize by higher knowledge whatever condition is so realizable and become an eyewitness in every case whatever the range may be should he wish to experience psychic power to hear devas sounds to read minds to call to mind previous dwellings to see beings faring on according to their deeds to enter and abide with the asavas destroyed in the emancipation by mind in the emancipation by wisdom he becomes an eyewitness in every case whatever the range may be that's the end of the sutta
commentary: in this case the buddha is again stressing the point of jhana concentration that by attaining concentration the janus you can attain the higher knowledges if you want the the last part was talking about the arbias the higher knowledges the ability to display psychic power to hear and deliver sounds sounds of spirits etc to read the minds of others to remember previous lifetimes and to see beings being born and dying and passing away according to karma and lastly to attain the destruction of the ah savasa and that can easily be attained with the right concentration and then in this utah the buddha gives the similes for the janus the first jana the buddha gave a simile of a person taking soap bath powder and squeezing it and putting water and gradually making it up into a ball so that the whole ball of powder becomes enveloped with moisture saturated with moisture every part of it every particle of it is saturated with water so in the same way the buddha is saying in the first jana every part of the body every particle of the body is affused with delight and pleasure pity and suka and secondly the buddha gave a simile of the second jhana of a pool and the pool in the bottom of the pool and the center bottom of the pool a spring comes up and the spring of cold water wells up and pervades every part of the lake and the same way this delight and pleasure wells up from within from within you and pervades every part of the body and the third jana the buddha gives a simile of a pond of water ladies which grow in the water and water is covering all them all over every part of them is covered by water in the same way a person the third ghana experiences pleasure without a delight without bt in every particle of the body and then the fourth jana the buddha gives a similaria of the person covered with a white cloth from the head down to the toes in the same way a person in the fourth jana the body is completely bright the mind is the brightness and pervades every part of the body and that from here you can understand why the fourth jhana is so important because some others would die you remember i said before and that the condition for inside the buddha is a bright mind when the mind is becomes very bright that is the best condition for attaining insight and wisdom that is why the fourth jhana is a very very good state for attaining wisdom and the last 1 is the contemplation sign the meditation sign is rightly reflected upon and so that wisdom arises now that is uh the arden right concentration [Music] now he comes with
